concept: Young / Early in Life / Not Yet Mature
description: Having lived or existed for only a short time; in an early stage of life or development. 'Limu' names the quality of being near the beginning—full of potential, not yet formed by time, carrying the energy of new life.
sound_symbolism: The word springs with new life. 'Li' begins with liquid aliveness, life starting. Then 'mu' holds new form, being not yet hardened.
grammatical_notes: Functions as an adjective: 'limu shiro' (young tree). Opposite of 'serao' (old). Related to potential, growth, and early stages of any development.

pillars.solarpunk-values: Young people inherit the future we create—their perspective and energy essential to building sustainable worlds.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Youth was both protected and prepared—the young carried forward the knowledge and practices of their people.
